metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5093
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1d-f: immunofluorescence imaging of CNK2 (gene CNKSR2) in cultured primary rat hippocampal neurons at DIV21 showed localization to dendritic spines. Double-labeling showed CNK2 co-localized with PSD-95 (DLG4) and staining was adjacent to presynaptic marker protein synapsin-1 (SYN1).

Based on the high quality imaging and because loss of CNK2 reduced PSD size (Fig. 2) and expression of a CNK2 variant that does not bind to the membrane affected PSD morphology (Fig. 3), this protein was annotated as PSD localized.

This protein was annotated as "extrinsic to the membrane" based on its attachment to the phospholipid membrane via the PH domain and the C-terminal stretch of the PH domain. Authors confirmed this using a EGFP-tagged CNK2 deletion construct that lacks the C-terminal region including the PH domain (Figure S2).. Experimental description: Results: "From immunofluorescence experiments on cultured primary rat hippocampal neurons at DIV21 we could see that endogenous CNK2 is enriched at postsynaptic sites in dendritic spines"

antibodies:
anti-CNK2 (guinea pig, Eurogentec, custom-made)
anti-CNK2 (rabbit, Sigma/Atlas, HPA 001502)
"As the commercially available antibodies did not meet expectations, we produced a custom-made antibody against all known CNK2 isoforms."
"The custom-made CNK2 antibody used in this study was produced by Eurogentec. It was raised in guinea pig against a KHL-conjugated peptide representing CNK2 amino-acids 727-741 and affinity matrix purified. The peptide sequence selected is present an all known CNK2 isoforms."

Cnk2A (Accession number: NM_177751.3) was amplified from cDNA generated from mouse brain. The pEGFP-C1-Cnk2 construct used in this study (Cnk2B, Accession number: NM_001374835.1) was cloned from the Cnk2A cDNA using the primers Cnk2-SalI-fw (aagtcgacatggctctgataatggaaccgg) and Cnk2-BamHI-rv (ttggatccttagcttttctctccaac).

We annotate the uniprot accession for the rat protein as it was endogenously targeted with (isoform unspecific) antibodies in Figure 1c. But could also have annotated the mouse protein which was overexpressed in Figure 1d (authors refer to NM_001374835 for the pEGFP-C1-Cnk2 construct, which is a mouse sequence).
